text: What is often called the 'Brain' of a computer?
fact: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.

text: What does AI stand for?
fact: AI helps computers 'think' and solve problems like humans.

text: Which of these is used by a drone to stay in the air?
What do we call a mistake in a computer program?
fact: The term came from a literal insect found in an old computer!

text: Which part of a computer 'remembers' things only while it is turned on?
What do we call the set of instructions that tells a robot what to do?
fact: The code is the software part of the robot.

text: How does a smartphone touch screen know where you are touching?
fact: Most modern screens use the electricity from your fingers.

text: What is the name for a computer program that can have a conversation with you?
fact: ChatGPT and Siri are examples of chatbots.

text: What part of a robot acts like its 'eyes'?
fact: Sensors help robots see, hear, and feel surroundings.

text: What is the global network of computers that connects us all?
fact: The Web is just one part of the Internet!

text: Which of these is a machine that can perform tasks automatically?
What does 'coding' mean?
fact: Coding is how we talk to computers.

text: What do you call the 'Cloud' where we store data?
options: A real cloud in the sky
A huge hard drive in your house
fact: The Cloud is just someone else's computer!

text: Which of these is a popular operating system for mobile phones?
fact: Android and iOS are the most common OS for phones.

text: What is an 'Algorithm'?
options: A step-by-step plan to solve a problem
answer: A step-by-step plan to solve a problem

text: What helps a computer connect to the internet without a cable?
fact: Wi-Fi uses radio waves to send data.

text: Which of these is used to 'print' a physical 3D object?
fact: It builds objects layer by layer with plastic or other materials.

text: What is 'Machine Learning'?
fact: This is how AI gets smarter over time.

text: What protects your computer from bad software?
What do we call the screen of a computer or phone?
fact: It's the output device that shows images.

text: Which of these can be controlled by a remote pilot?
fact: Pilots use controllers or tablets to fly drones.

text: What do you use to save your photos and games permanently?
fact: Unlike RAM, storage keeps data when power is off.

text: What is a 'Web Browser'?
fact: Chrome, Safari, and Edge are browsers.

text: What part of a computer keeps the parts cool?
fact: Computers get hot when they work hard!

text: What is 'Virtual Reality' (VR)?
fact: You usually wear a headset to see it.

text: What do you call a person who writes computer code?
fact: Programmers use languages like Python or Scratch.

text: Which of these is a way to prove you are the owner of an account?
fact: Passwords should be kept secret and strong.

text: What is 'Binary' code?
fact: Computers use electricity (on/off) to read 0s and 1s.

text: What do we call the 'intelligence' of a self-driving car?
fact: AI helps the car recognize roads and stop signs.

text: What is a 'Pixel'?
options: A small dot on a screen
answer: A small dot on a screen
fact: Thousands of pixels make up an image.

text: Which part of a smartphone takes photos?
fact: Light enters through the lens to the sensor.

text: What is an 'App' short for?
fact: An app is a computer program for a specific task.

text: How does a robot know it has bumped into a wall?
fact: Sensors send a signal back to the robot's brain.

text: What is the main circuit board of a computer called?
fact: It connects all the parts together.

text: What do we call copying data from the internet to your computer?
fact: Uploading is sending data FROM your computer.

text: What is 'Cybersecurity'?
options: Protecting computers and data from hackers
answer: Protecting computers and data from hackers

text: Which of these is a coding language designed for kids?
fact: Scratch uses blocks to build code.

text: What do we call a small computer you can carry in your pocket?
fact: It's a computer, phone, and camera in one.

text: Which of these lets a computer talk to another computer far away?
What is 'Streaming'?
options: Playing a game from a CD
Watching video or listening to music from the internet directly
Drawing in a stream of water
answer: Watching video or listening to music from the internet directly
fact: YouTube and Netflix are streaming services.

text: What tells a computer which pixels to turn on to show a game?
fact: The GPU handles all the visuals.

text: What keeps a laptop running when it isn't plugged in?
What is a 'Desktop' computer?
options: A computer meant to stay in one place on a desk
A computer you carry in your pocket
answer: A computer meant to stay in one place on a desk
fact: They are usually more powerful than laptops.

text: What do we call the pointer you see on a computer screen?
fact: You move the cursor with your mouse or trackpad.

text: What is an 'Emoji'?
options: A digital symbol used to show feeling
answer: A digital symbol used to show feeling
fact: Emojis help us communicate emotion in text.

text: Which of these is a social network?
fact: They allow you to share updates with friends.

text: How do robots move their 'arms'?
What is 'encryption'?
options: Scrambling a message so only the receiver can read it
answer: Scrambling a message so only the receiver can read it

text: What part of a phone vibrates?
fact: It's a tiny motor that spins off-balance.

text: What do we call the address of a website (like google.com)?
fact: URL stands for Uniform Resource Locator.

text: What is a 'Laptop'?
Which company created the iPhone?
fact: The first iPhone came out in 2007.

text: What is the largest land animal?
fact: They can weigh up to 6 tons!

text: Which bird is known for its ability to mimic human speech?
fact: African Greys are particularly good at it.

text: How many chambers are in a human heart?
fact: The heart has two atria and two ventricles.

text: Which animal is known as the 'King of the Jungle'?
What do bees collect from flowers to make honey?
fact: They store nectar in their second stomach.

text: What is a baby kangaroo called?
fact: Joeys stay in the mother's pouch for months.

text: Which organ in the human body is responsible for breathing?
fact: Lungs take in oxygen and release CO2.

text: How many legs does a spider have?
What do we call animals that sleep during the day and are active at night?
Which gas do plants create during photosynthesis?
fact: Plants breathe in CO2 and breathe out Oxygen.

text: What has to be broken before you can use it?
fact: You have to crack it to cook it!

text: I’m tall when I’m young, and I’m short when I’m old. What am I?
What month of the year has 28 days?
fact: Every month has at least 28 days.

text: What is full of holes but still holds water?
What can you catch, but not throw?
fact: You catch a sickness, but don't throw it!

text: What goes up but never comes down?
fact: You only get older, never younger.

text: What starts with T, ends with T, and has T in it?
If an electric train is traveling south, which way is the smoke going?
What has a face and two hands but no arms or legs?
fact: A clock's hands tell the time.

text: What has many keys but can’t open a single lock?
What belongs to you, but everyone else uses it more?
fact: People call you by your name.

text: What stays in one place but travels around the world?
fact: A stamp stays on the corner of the envelope.

text: I have branches, but no fruit, trunk, or leaves. What am I?
What has a neck but no head?
fact: These items all have parts called 'necks'.

text: What has four legs but can’t walk?
The more of this there is, the less you see. What is it?
fact: In total darkness, you see nothing.

text: What gets wet while drying?
fact: It dries you and gets wet itself.

text: What has one eye but can’t see?
If you drop me I’m sure to crack, but give me a smile and I’ll always smile back. What am I?
I’m always in front of you but can’t be seen. What am I?
